blinding.annotator_visible_files: NONE of these files is handed to annotators; sheets are generated per annotation-protocol.md

composition_rationale.decidability: with the pinned analysis (analysis/g3.py, z=1.645 Wilson at n=200): PASS (ub<=0.10) needs <=13 confirmed violations; FAIL (lb>0.10) needs >=27; each 60-instance stress lane makes both branches reachable (a 20-instance lane could never trip FAIL on its own)

concept_selection.rule: non-knownWeak kernel-v0 concepts (knownWeak per data/kernel-v0/manifest.json), frame-stratified quotas InstanceSchema 6 / WhenTrue 6 / RelationalSchema 8 (proportional to the corpus's 17/16/21), ranked ascending by sha256('kot-g3-mat/1|seed=0|' + slug) hex; 'promise' force-included in InstanceSchema if unranked (not triggered - it ranks 4th of 9)

for_experiment.corpus_pin_placeholder: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:g3.materials (ops amendment computes the kot-corpus-hash/1 digest of this directory before any final-phase run)

human_pass_decides: Q1 per instance: is the target claim true of the described situation under ordinary usage (instance-of-C)?
Q2 per instance: does the described situation satisfy EVERY condition in the concept's condition set (bindings as given)?
necessity violation := Q1 yes AND Q2 no; sufficiency violation := Q1 no AND Q2 yes (P1 HS3 / F7 verbatim definitions)

ordering.condition_set_ids: K-01..K-20 in block order (opaque, for concept-word-free pass-B sheets)
ordering.instance_ids: g3-<slug>-<NN> assigned AFTER the shuffle, so id order carries no stratum signal

scrub_rule: instance text contains neither the concept word nor a close derivative (per-concept banned-lemma regexes in the generator); the target claim and bindings are exempt - they state the judgment

source_kernel.stimulus_source_field: gloss (the human-readable mirror of the kot-ast/1 explication)

title: G3 candidate materials: necessity-vs-sufficiency violation set (~20 concepts x 10 instances)
